reviews.review_msg: So lovely to work with, difficult grader but feedback reflects grades and she is genuinely invested in your learning and well-being. I love Cathy!

She was definitely a very caring and approachable prof. The lectures can get a little dry at times, but that's the struggles you face in 3 hour long classes. Be prepared that there is a LOT of material to study for the Midterm exam, but her exams are very fair and she provides ample time to complete them.

I've had 2 classes with her and she is so sweet and accommodating! 3-hour lectures with 2 five-min breaks is crazy, but there is a lot of content she has to get through. The midterms are hard since you have a lot to memorize but the final is take-home. always responds to emails and eager to help in any way. great person, but hard classes

Seems like a very kind person who cares about her students, but the midterm was so brutal (at least 20 pages of writing in 2.5 hours) that the average grade was below 50. I fear I would not take another course with her again. Grades were often returned late, and she was often very unclear with deadlines and expectations around them.

She seems like a nice professor, however, she is not very engaging when teaching. She would teach for about an hour out of a three hour class, wth the remaining two hours of the class consist of watching a film. Also, the class was mostly guest lectures. If possible, take ARCH 375 with another prof, I would NOT recommend this class.

Perhaps once this prof was passionate and articulate like I hear some people describe her as, however for the past couple years the only term applied to her is "scattered". Dates are often wrong and contradictory on slides, she consistently forgets deadlines, and due to her rambling the class moves at a snail's pace through exceedingly dry material
The best prof ever!

She made me fall in love with arch! Be prepared to attend lecture and put in work. I did read the textbooks and found them to be helpful but focus on the lecture material more for the exams. She is passionate about her work and is a brilliant instructor. I always visited her in office hours and got a B+  in the class.
Boring and uninformative lectures as she simply reads her slides and does not give feedbacks on tour assignments. Would never take a course with her again.
She is super passionate  and knowledgeable about the course.  She is also very clear with what is going to be on the exam. Great prof!

Not a big fan of her teaching and tests. There are only 3 tests the whole term and the first two are practically the same yet one is worth 50% and the other only 30%.We also received a take home essay but it is useless because we are being asked to basically repeat exactly what she said.

I actually like D'Andrea - she is helpful and approachable, and seems to care about the students. My biggest issue with this course is the exams. Requires memorization of hundreds of terms/people/ideas and if you have forgotten a small handful it is possible to fail, as the scope of the exams is rather limited.

Her tests are ridiculous. Matching, definitions, and essay questions. You are required to learn and memorize over 150 terms at least for each test, yet she only tests you on 5 or 6. No list of specific terms to study, anything is game. Asks questions on terms she spends very little time on. At least give us a list of terms to study.
You should take this

Catherine is an amazing prof. She is extremely interested in archaeology and if you allow her to, she will get you interested in the subject as well. In order to do well, go to all of her lectures (when the TA's aren't teaching) and take notes for all slides. This class has made me contemplate doing a minor in ARCH.

If you go through the readings and take really great notes (as in, write down basically everything she says) and then study those notes, you will find it easy to get an A+. Just skim over the readings if you want (know the bold terms), though there weren't many exam questions on the readings.
She is a good prof. Lecture is quite boring. The grades marked by 4 quizs. If u read her lecture notes you can get B, if u can spend time on textbook, trust me, easy A.

She's a great professor who really cares for her students. When we fell behind, Catherine cut her notes down instead of rushing through material. Although the readings were supposedly examinable, I haven't done any and I've managed an average above 90%. So, use the recordings, take detailed notes, memorize as much as you can and you'll get an A+.

Easy course, just lots of memorization for quizzes. no mid, final, or assignments. Just skim the text, most of it covered in lecture. SFU should just abolish these breadth courses, as they are ridiculous for both the students pursuing a completely different discipline and the profs who have to teach students who have no interest in the material.

Had no sense of timing. Would rush through lectures, jumping around slides, only to dismiss the class an hour early. Would also dismiss class if she sensed lack of interest, which led to falling an entire 3-hour lecture behind. I felt extremely frustrated most of the time. Tests are fairly easy, all multiple-choice. Doesn't feel approachable.
Extremely helpful and engaging. Clear, concise lectures.

Really good class... Pretty easy if you go to the lectures/ listen to them online. GREAT powerpoint slides! Was very clear about what was going to be on exams. If you have any interest in ancient Egypt or Africa definitely take this class.
She really cares about her area of expertise and it shows. She can be tough, but she is fair and will go out of her way to help you if you need it. And on top of that, I find her very attractive.

Cathy was a great professor - her lectures were definitely long, but were made interesting with the incorporation of pictures and some videos. If you're interested in the archaeology of ancient Egypt, this is the class to get some more background knowledge. The three tests were pretty easy, except for the essays, which I felt she marked too hard.

Heavy on the readings, 60-90 slides per 3hr lecture, hard marker & some tests are unusually difficult (inconsistent with emphasized points in lecture). Seriously impossible to ace the tests unless you memorize *everything* including random details. Even if your interest level is high, AVOID THIS CLASS like you would the bubonic plague.
Very boring lectures, three hours of blah! Exams are unfair, barely any choice and quite a few obscure terms.Marks hard also. Loved the topic but she ruined it. Would avoid her in the future.

She is a fair prof who is very approachable and understanding. Her notes are clear and interesting - she provides lots of pictures to help understand as well as enhance our knowledge. Her exams are multiple choice.. as long as you study all her notes and read the textbook you are gauranteed an A!

This class was pretty easy. Didnt ever have to attend lecture as she posted powerpoint and recordings online. All you have to do is make sure you read the powerpoints and the text and you're guaranteed at least a B in this class. The only problem was that the tests were all multiple choice(which i dont prefer, but some of you may like it)

She is a good prof.  She knows what she is talking about and fills the lectures with lots of interesting information.  But her test format is quite difficult.  When she had multiple choice everyone excelled, when she got rid of it, everyone failed.  Make sure you know the stuff inside and out, otherwise you're screwed.
Very well organized. Kind of boring, but overall she knows her stuff. Veteran prof. Study tip: NVM the readings; take notes in lecture, and just use slds= B+ to an A+

LOVED this class--amazing prof, extremely helpful if you go to her office hours, kept the 3hr lectures interesting.  The exams (were only 3 exams for entire grade) are difficult, but not if you study and know the lecture material WELL.  Definitely recommend the course!

Apart from the fact that she is a really nice person, her teaching style is not effective at all. her tests are unfair and not clear. and her grading system is really rough. i personally, got bored during the lectures and felt that this is one of the most useless courses I've ever taken.

She's very helpful and a great ethnobotanist. However, unless you can memorize hundreds of seed for what they look like (not from a book) and their latin name then you will not do well in this class no matter how hard you work. You won't learn much about archaeobotany other then you can get eye strain from the minut seeds you'll be staring at.
Cathy is fairly interesting to listen to. The lectures are three hours long so sometimes boredom sets in, but for the most part she keeps my attention. The midterms are difficult but fair.

soso lol.hav to memorize a lot of thing.well..just memory the lecture notes is enough+lec tape..but. to get good on essay question. guess hav to study more on txtbook and video bah..we hav 2 essay question but there is one that is about video!!!(2003fall)
Her lectures are clear informative and fun, but they do not help at all with the midterms.  She often "hints" at possible midterm questions and then doesn't put them on the midterm.
Cathy is great! She brought homebaked goods to all our botany lectures! Her tests are easy if you study and learn her style. She is super helpful with papers, particularly if you like seeds.

She may be really nice but shes really not a very interesting lecturer.  Tests are tough but if you study you should do fine.  But if you want to stay awake during class take this course with a different prof.

Her material is interesting, yet she neither detracts from its appeal nor adds to it. Seeing her about your essay will get you a "you're on the right track" and not much else. She seems nice enough, though.

Well she's a nice person but don't let the "no tutorial/hw and 4 tests" system fool you.  It's not easy to get an A cuz tests are marked pretty hard. Her classes are quite boring and there's just way too much info to memorize. Personally, I really hate this class.

I love Cathy's class, even though the lectures are non interactive. She's so into her topic and she really makes it easy to follow along with her webCT notes and slides. My only complaint is that her tests are really hard.
Catherine is fabulous! She is brilliant, and driven and very devoted to her work.  I agree that exams are hard, but fair.  She appreciates creative/intelligent paper topics.  Take Egypt/Africa!
Very dry lectures. Not too hard though. And shes extremely nice if talking one on one. A shame the classes were so monotonous
Super nice and helpful.  Very clear and put together lectures, power point was awesome!   Easy to follow and open to helping as much as she could!  Rad Prof!

Gives lots of information and communicates it very clearly.  Obviously interested in the subject but her tone of voice when doing the lectures is a bit drousy.  Exams really hard so make sure you study!

Brutal exams; matching questions are tricky - study lots.  Lectures aren't exciting, no class interaction.  Overall, a very nice instructor. Take a course with her in a subject that you're interested in and she's interested in - Egypt/Africa.

if it's interest in a subject you want from a prof, Dr. D'Andrea's it. organized, she'll tell you what she expects, tests are hard so do the readings, watch the videos! she's just good at what she does, easy to approach.
Great instructor. Very helpful and willing to talk and provide direction.

GREAT teacher, very interested in subject matter.  Great slides, very helpful, and course material is very clear to understand!!!  Have also taken Arch.100 with her as well, and would love to take more classes with her.
Wonderful prof.
She is informative but not terribly exciting, certainly not interesting enough to liven up a class with boring subject matter.  The lectures were difficult to sit through.
Great Class. Great Teacher.
Very good teacher. Disragard others bad comments. She expects students to be analytical and self-motivated; if you get a bad mark its because you aren't able to take your learning to a higher level.
An OK prof. The multiple choice section of her exams were brutal. The only thing that salvaged my mark was my term paper.
HARD exams!!! However, she's a good instructor and knows her stuff.
Good Professor.  If you have an interest in what she is teaching than she is interesting.  Has an interest in what she teaches, this is very important to me.
appears knowledgeable but very dry lectures, don't get on her bad side.No time for students.
Good teacher, very helpful

Not bad--interesting enough so that you don't bang your head against a brick wall halfway through a 3 hour lectures.  Tests aren't too hard; tries to schedule tests during as non-busy weeks as possible.

I took this class as an elective.  I can honestly say that having graduated with two undergrad degrees and now working on a master's degree that this woman should not be teaching.  She might be intelligent but a PhD. does not that mean one can teach...
she was very nice and helpful
No Comments
Excellent teacher. Extremely intersted in helping undergraduates.
She is such a nice prof.  2 hours lectures and 1 hour video.  Although she talks kinda fast, but if u go listen to the lecture tape, the lecture will be very clear.
I hear she's not bad in other courses but do not take 272 with her!
perfectly mediochre in every way
really knows her stuff, and keeps it interesting.
She's not that bad, she really knows her stuff and that's important!
Hard marker, unclear lectures-to fast, too much material.
Terrible, Unhelpful, always off topic, too much material for course
